page: Amazon Web Services r5.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the r5.xlarge exhibits modest compute capabilities. In stress-ng and Geekbench tests, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, along with data compression and decompression tasks, rank in the weak or poor categories. However, the PassMark memory score achieves an average rating, placing it in the middle 50% of tested systems, which reflects its memory-centric design despite the lower compute throughput.

The primary tradeoff of the r5.xlarge is its high memory capacity paired with relatively low compute and network performance. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require intensive CPU processing or high-speed local storage, such as caching layers, medium-sized databases, and memory-resident applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r5.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro System, this instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate modest compute capabilities, with CPU and memory bandwidth tests generally ranking in the bottom 25% tier, though its PassMark memory score reaches the middle 50% average. With a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, the r5.xlarge is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high CPU throughput, such as caching layers, in-memory databases, and data-processing applications requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads with modest compute requirements.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for workloads requiring larger memory footprints without intensive compute demands.

32.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
No local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average memory performance paired with lower-tier compute benchmarks
Suited for caching layers, in-memory databases, and memory-resident applications
